title: Combining RPA and DNA-templated reaction for SARS-CoV-2 sensing with dual fluorescence and LFA output
author: Farrera-Soler, Lluc
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The early phase of the SARS-CoV-2 pandemic was exacerbated by a diagnostic challenge of unprecedented magnitude.  In the absence of effective therapeutics or vaccines, breaking the chain of transmission through early disease detection and patient isolation was the only means to control the growing pandemic.  While PCR-based methods and rapid-antigen tests rose to the occasion, the analytical challenge of rapid sequence-specific nucleic acid-sensing at a point-of-care or home setting stimulated intense developments.  Herein we report a method that combines recombinase polymerase amplification (RPA) and a DNA- templated reaction to achieve a dual readout with either fluorescence or lateral flow assay (LFA) detection.  The nucleic acid templated reaction is based on an S N Ar that simultaneously transfers biotin from one PNA strand to another PNA strand enabling LFA detection and uncages a coumarin for fluorescence readout.  This methodology has been applied to detect a DNA sequence uniquely attributed to the SARS-CoV-2.
steps_raw: The early phase of the SARS-CoV-2 pandemic was exacerbated by a diagnostic challenge of unprecedented magnitude.  In the absence of effective therapeutics or vaccines, breaking the chain of transmission through early disease detection and patient isolation was the only means to control the growing pandemic.  While PCR-based methods and rapid-antigen tests rose to the occasion, the analytical challenge of rapid sequence-specific nucleic acid-sensing at a point-of-care or home setting stimulated intense developments.  Herein we report a method that combines recombinase polymerase amplification (RPA) and a DNA- templated reaction to achieve a dual readout with either fluorescence or lateral flow assay (LFA) detection.  The nucleic acid templated reaction is based on an S N Ar that simultaneously transfers biotin from one PNA strand to another PNA strand enabling LFA detection and uncages a coumarin for fluorescence readout.  This methodology has been applied to detect a DNA sequence uniquely attributed to the SARS-CoV-2.
